manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: tick, card, tube, eye, bed, shakespeare, belt, park, lap.
Your opponent's team words are: poison, cotton, air, dress, wake, apple, hotel, olive.
Distractor words are: well, amazon, fork.
The kill words are: sink, grace, spine, seal, mercury.

message.reasoning:
We must give a clue and list of target words from our team's words. Must be a single word not any of the words listed. Should be semantically related to some of our words. Choose clue that links multiple words. Our words: tick, card, tube, eye, bed, shakespeare, belt, park, lap.

We need produce only: "CLUE: <WORD>" line and "TARGETS: <TARGETS>" line. No extra text.

We need to ensure clue not using any of the words. Also can't contain parts. For example "reading" is part of "read"? Not word but part. Better avoid.

We can pick "night" which relates to "bed", "park" (night park?), "eye" (night eye?), maybe "tick" (night tick?). Might be ambiguous.

Better choose "Royal" linking "Shakespeare" (Elizabethan) and "belt" (royal belt?), not great.

Maybe "London" linking "Shakespeare" (plays set), "tube" (London Underground), "park" (London parks). That's three. Not using any list words. London is okay. Not in list. So clue "London" and targets: tube, shakespeare, park. Also maybe "bed" (London bed?). No.

Check that "London" is not part of any word: not.

Thus output:

CLUE: London
TARGETS: tube, shakespeare, park

Make sure format exactly
